─[ CI Lint Scans Checkout Dirs ]─ ┐

name: ci-lint-scans-checkout-dirs description: Prevent ESLint and Prettier from scanning repos checked out into subdirectories by CI workflows user-invocable: false allowed-tools: Read, Edit, Write

CI Lint Scans Checkout Dirs

When a CI workflow checks out a second repo into a subdirectory (e.g., actions/checkout with path: lore), linters will scan that directory too. This causes false failures: ESM files get CommonJS parse errors, browser globals trigger no-undef, and Prettier finds formatting mismatches across a foreign codebase.

Detection

CI lint step fails with errors in files that aren’t your own code. Look for foreign paths in the error output — e.g., lore/.opencode/plugins/ or lore/docs/javascripts/. If you didn’t write those files, a checkout dir is being scanned.

Fix

Prettier — add the directory to .prettierignore:

lore/

ESLint flat config (eslint.config.js) — add an ignore block as the first entry:

export default [
  { ignores: ['lore/'] },
  // ...rest of config
];

ESLint legacy (.eslintignore) — add the directory path:

lore/

Snags

  • The ignore entry must be the first item in eslint.config.js flat config arrays — later entries can re-enable files if a config spread applies first.
  • Trailing slash is required in flat config ignores to match the directory and all its contents.
  • Both Prettier and ESLint need separate fixes; fixing one leaves the other still failing.
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
